title: 1.1: States that during config parsing, module instances are created via factory functions (e.g., RegisterInstance stores the module and config together without calling Init).
1.2: States that module Init calls are deferred until the module is actually needed (e.g., Init only invoked when GetInstance is called).
1.3: Describes that the ampersand prefix triggers GetInstance which performs lazy initialization of the referenced module (e.g., strings.HasPrefix detection leading to GetInstance lookup).
1.4: Describes that the Initialized map in GetInstance prevents circular dependency loops (e.g., Initialized[name] set to true before calling Init).
1.5: Identifies that endpoint modules are not placed in the global instances registry (e.g., collected into separate endpoints slice via GetEndpoint).
1.6: Explains that endpoint modules are initialized first because this drives the lazy initialization cascade of modules they reference (e.g., endpoint Init triggers GetInstance for storage modules).
1.7: Describes that check modules coordinate by having their state objects created per-message during pipeline processing (e.g., check state initialized when message arrives).
1.8: Describes that modifier modules coordinate through a multi-level processing chain (e.g., global, per-source, and per-recipient modifier levels).
1.9: Identifies that endpoint listening messages serve as runtime evidence the module graph has fully settled (e.g., "smtp: listening on..." and "imap: listening on...").
